description: Phase 1 — Establish the token-based SCSS design system: ocean/sand colour palette as CSS custom properties, 4px spacing grid, system font stack, z-index scale, base reset, breakpoint and focus-visible mixins, main.scss entry point, and a /design preview route via Vue Router.

userStories.title: SCSS scaffolding and colour tokens
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the SCSS directory structure created and all colour tokens defined as CSS custom properties so that every subsequent token and component file has a consistent home and palette to reference.
acceptanceCriteria: sass installed as a devDependency in ui/ via yarn
ui/src/styles/ directory created with subdirectories: tokens/, mixins/, base/, utilities/

ui/src/styles/tokens/_colors.scss created with all palette tokens inside :root {}: ocean (50–900), sand (50–900), neutral (0–900), surf score semantics (--color-surf-very-good: #2d9e5f, --color-surf-good: #1a72c4, --color-surf-maybe: #d4a017, --color-surf-poor: #c0392b), and UI aliases (--color-background, --color-surface, --color-text-primary, --color-text-secondary, --color-border, --color-primary, --color-accent) referencing palette tokens via var()
No hard-coded hex values appear outside _colors.scss
Typecheck passes (yarn typecheck)
userStories.notes: Exact hex values are specified in the PRD — use them verbatim. Surf score colours are standalone hex values, not aliased to the palette.

userStories.title: Spacing, typography, and z-index tokens
userStories.description: As a developer, I want spacing, typography, and z-index values available as CSS custom properties so that all layout and text decisions reference a shared scale.
acceptanceCriteria: ui/src/styles/tokens/_spacing.scss created, defining --space-1 (4px) through --space-12 (48px) in 4px increments inside :root {}

ui/src/styles/tokens/_typography.scss created, defining --font-family-base (-apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif), --font-size-xs (0.75rem) through --font-size-4xl (2.25rem), --font-weight-regular (400) through --font-weight-bold (700), --line-height-tight (1.2), --line-height-normal (1.5), --line-height-relaxed (1.75) inside :root {}
ui/src/styles/tokens/_z-index.scss created, defining --z-below (-1), --z-base (0), --z-raised (10), --z-dropdown (100), --z-overlay (200), --z-modal (300), --z-toast (400) inside :root {}
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Base reset and global styles
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a CSS reset and sensible global defaults applied so that the browser's user-agent stylesheet does not interfere with the design system.
acceptanceCriteria: ui/src/styles/base/_reset.scss created: *, *::before, *::after { box-sizing: border-box }, margin and padding zeroed on all elements, img and video set to max-width: 100% and display: block

ui/src/styles/base/_base.scss created: html sets font-size: 16px; body uses --font-family-base, --font-size-md, --line-height-normal, background-color: var(--color-background), color: var(--color-text-primary); a elements inherit color by default
No component-specific styles in these files — element-level defaults only
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Utility mixins — breakpoints and focus-visible
userStories.description: As a developer, I want reusable SCSS mixins for responsive breakpoints and focus states so I can apply them consistently without repeating media query values.
acceptanceCriteria: ui/src/styles/mixins/_breakpoints.scss created with a breakpoint($size) mixin using mobile-first min-width media queries: sm=480px, md=768px, lg=1024px, xl=1280px; @error thrown for unknown size names

ui/src/styles/mixins/_focus.scss created with a focus-visible-ring mixin that applies outline: 2px solid var(--color-primary) with outline-offset: 2px on :focus-visible and removes outline on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
Both files output no CSS when imported — only when @included
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use SCSS $variables for breakpoint values (not CSS custom properties) — custom properties cannot be used inside @media queries.

userStories.title: main.scss entry point
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a single main.scss that imports all token and base files so that one import in main.ts loads the entire design system.
acceptanceCriteria: ui/src/styles/main.scss created, importing in order: tokens/_colors, tokens/_spacing, tokens/_typography, tokens/_z-index, base/_reset, base/_base using @use or @forward
ui/src/main.ts imports './styles/main.scss'
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: open DevTools → Elements → :root and confirm --color-primary, --space-4, and --font-size-md are present as CSS custom properties
userStories.notes: Use @use syntax (not deprecated @import). Order matters: tokens before base, as base files reference token custom properties.

userStories.title: Vue Router setup and /design preview route
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a /design route that renders all design tokens visually so I can verify the system is wired correctly at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: vue-router installed as a dependency in ui/ via yarn add vue-router

ui/src/router.ts created with two routes: / → HomeView (src/views/HomeView.vue, renders
Home
), /design → DesignPreview (src/views/DesignPreview.vue)
ui/src/main.ts registers the router via app.use(router)
App.vue updated to render <RouterView />

DesignPreview.vue contains four clearly labelled sections: (1) Colour palette — one swatch per token group (ocean, sand, neutral, surf score, UI aliases) showing token name and a filled colour box; (2) Spacing scale — a horizontal bar per step --space-1 through --space-12 with token name and pixel value; (3) Typography — each --font-size-* rendered as sample text 'The quick brown fox' labelled with its token name; (4) Z-index — a table of token name and value
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: navigate to http://localhost:5173/design and confirm all four sections (colours, spacing, typography, z-index) render correctly
userStories.notes: vue-router v4 required (Vue 3 compatible). The /design route is a developer tool — no auth guard needed.
